expectations.text: report_has_structured_format: Report contains the Phase 5 format with a 'Coherence Check Report' header and at least two of: 'Auto-Fixed', 'Needs Manual Review', 'Verified Clean', 'Coverage' sections
expectations.evidence: Report has all four Phase 5 sections: '## Coherence Check Report' header, 'Auto-Fixed' (4 items), 'Needs Manual Review' (2 items), 'Verified Clean' (13 items), 'Coverage' (19+ files listed)

expectations.text: checks_pipeline_membership: Report explicitly verifies whether WebSocketProber (or websocket_prober) appears in workers/info_gathering/pipeline.py in a stage's tools list
expectations.evidence: Verified Clean section: 'Worker pipeline — pipeline.py: WebSocketProber imported and placed in identify_entry_points stage. EntryPointAggregator placed in aggregate_entry_points stage.'

expectations.text: checks_playbooks_sync: Report explicitly checks shared/lib_webbh/playbooks.py PIPELINE_STAGES for stage name consistency with pipeline.py
expectations.evidence: Auto-Fixed section: 'aggregate_entry_points missing from PIPELINE_STAGES["info_gathering"] in playbooks' and 'review_comments_deep missing from PIPELINE_STAGES["info_gathering"] in playbooks' — both found and fixed in shared/lib_webbh/playbooks.py

expectations.text: checks_concurrency_weight: Report explicitly checks workers/info_gathering/concurrency.py for a WeightClass assignment covering the WebSocketProber tool
expectations.evidence: Verified Clean section: 'Worker concurrency — concurrency.py: WebSocketProber registered as LIGHT — correct (aiohttp HTTP, not subprocess). EntryPointAggregator registered as LIGHT — correct. No stale tool references.'

expectations.text: checks_lib_webbh_imports: Report verifies that lib_webbh symbols imported in the tool file exist in shared/lib_webbh/__init__.py
expectations.evidence: Verified Clean section: 'Shared lib — lib_webbh/__init__.py: All symbols imported by the three tools (Asset, Parameter, get_session) present in __all__. No stale import names.'

expectations.text: coverage_section_lists_files: The Coverage section (or equivalent) names at least 5 specific file paths that were actually inspected during the check
expectations.evidence: Coverage section explicitly lists 19 specific file paths including workers/info_gathering/tools/websocket_prober.py, pipeline.py, concurrency.py, main.py, base_tool.py, shared/lib_webbh/playbooks.py, __init__.py, database.py, schema.sql, dashboard/src/lib/worker-stages.ts, orchestrator/main.py, and more

claims.claim: 4 auto-fixes were applied to real files (playbooks.py, worker-stages.ts, WorkflowBuilder.tsx)
claims.evidence: Auto-Fixed section lists 4 specific fixes with file paths and line numbers. Files are confirmed present in codebase.
Report header states 'Checks performed: 28' and Coverage section lists files that correspond to distinct check categories

claims.claim: review_comments_deep re-runs identical tool set as review_comments with no differentiation
claims.evidence: Needs Manual Review item 2 shows both stages have same five tools (CommentHarvester, MetadataExtractor, JsSecretScanner, SourceMapProber, RedirectBodyInspector) with same section_id=4.1.5

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether auto-fixes were actually written to disk (Edit tool used) vs merely described. The with-skill agent applied 4 real fixes but an assertion verifying the fix landed in the file would catch cases where an agent describes fixes it didn't actually make.

No assertion checks whether the agent flags test assertion staleness (test_playbooks.py stage count == 10 vs actual 12). The without-skill agent caught this; the with-skill agent missed it. This is a meaningful gap in the skill's ripple map.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-calibrated. The auto-fix verification gap is the most actionable improvement.
